sanskrit: अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः । स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ॥
hindiTranslation: हे कृष्ण अधर्म के अधिक बढ़ जाने से कुल की स्त्रियाँ दूषित हो जाती हैं और हे वार्ष्णेय स्त्रियों के दूषित होने पर वर्णसंकर पैदा हो जाते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: When lawlessness grows, the women of the family are corrupted. When women are corrupted, mixed social disorder is born.
essence: When order breaks, the damage spreads into future generations.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna fears that when lawlessness takes over a family, women are harmed and social order breaks down further.
simpleInsight: Moral collapse feels frightening because it spreads beyond one person and damages the whole community.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is no longer thinking like a warrior. He is imagining the collapse of family order, the loss of restraint, and the damage that war may bring to future generations. This verse continues his fearful argument against fighting.
modernRelevance: A family argument turns ugly, and suddenly everyone is carrying the damage. One harsh decision can echo into children, trust, and the whole home.
translationLiteral: अधर्माभिभवात् (from the dominance of lawlessness) / कृष्ण (O Krishna) / प्रदुष्यन्ति (become corrupted) / कुलस्त्रियः (family women) / स्त्रीषु (when women) / दुष्टासु (are corrupted) / वार्ष्णेय (O descendant of Vrishni) / जायते (is born) / वर्णसङ्करः (mixed disorder of social order)
insight: It shifts attention from personal victory to the wider social cost of destructive action.
